process_name: Exclusive B -> K ell+ ell- branching fractions

evidence: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 as modern phenomenology systems.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 implements check_mu_to_e_gamma; no rare B semileptonic backend is present in flavorConstraints.

Required greps over quarkConstraints, qcd, flavorConstraints, neutrinos, yukawa, warpConfig, solvers, scanParams, and tests found Delta F = 2 and mu -> e gamma support, but no B -> K ell ell, b -> s ell ell, C9, C10, or R_K implementation.
commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n -e 'B_to_K' -e 'BtoK' -e 'B -> K' -e 'B\\s*to\\s*K' -e 'b.?->.?s.?ell' -e 'C9' -e 'C10' -e 'R_K' -e 'Bplus' -e 'Kplus' quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(B\to K\ell^+\ell^-\), with \(\ell=e,\mu\).
This entry covers the exclusive pseudoscalar-kaon \(b\to s\ell^+\ell^-\)
branching fractions, centered on \(B^+\to K^+\ell^+\ell^-\) and
\(B^0\to K^0\ell^+\ell^-\).  Lepton-universality ratios are cataloged in
the companion \(B018\) entry, and vector-kaon angular observables in \(B017\).
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical measured values are the HFLAV rare-\(B\) averages from the end
of December 2025, accessed on 2026-05-16 and snapshotted locally under
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B016/}.  For source key
\texttt{HFLAV2025Dec:BplusToKplusll}, HFLAV quotes
\[
  {\cal B}(B^+\to K^+\ell^+\ell^-)
  =(5.76\pm0.40)\times10^{-7},
\]
and for source key \texttt{HFLAV2025Dec:B0ToK0ll},
\[
  {\cal B}(B^0\to K^0\ell^+\ell^-)
  =(3.28\pm0.32)\times10^{-7}.
\]
Both values are recorded in \texttt{B016.yaml} under
\texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent}.  HFLAV notes that treatments of charmonium
intermediate components differ among inputs.
sections.relevance:
This is a \(\Delta B=1\) flavor-changing neutral-current probe of the same
down-sector flavor structure that appears in \(B_s\) mixing, but it tests
semileptonic and dipole operators rather than four-quark \(\Delta F=2\)
operators.  In warped or anarchic-flavor models, flavor-changing \(Z\) or KK
electroweak-gauge couplings and loop dipoles can feed \(C_7\), \(C_9\),
\(C_{10}\), and primed semileptonic coefficients.  The branching fractions are
therefore useful diagnostics of \(b\to s\ell\ell\) structure, while the LFU
ratios and \(K^*\) angular program carry related but distinct information.
sections.post_2008:
Since the arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline, source key
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}, this channel moved from
sparse \(B\)-factory evidence into precision rare-\(B\) phenomenology.  BaBar
provided early post-baseline \(B\to K^{(*)}\ell^+\ell^-\) asymmetry inputs
(\texttt{BaBar2009:BtoKllAsymmetries}).  LHCb measured differential
\(B\to K^{(*)}\mu^+\mu^-\) branching fractions and isospin asymmetries with
hadron-collider data (\texttt{LHCb2014:BtoKmumuDifferential}), and Belle
measured both electron and muon \(B\to K\ell\ell\) modes together with \(R_K\)
(\texttt{Belle2021:BtoKllLFU}).  HFLAV now provides current charged and
neutral exclusive-\(K\) branching-fraction averages, while the separate \(R_K\)
updates shifted the clean LFU-anomaly interpretation toward correlated
semileptonic-Wilson-coefficient and hadronic-systematics questions.
sections.validity:
The HFLAV branching fractions are measured observables and are appropriate for
cataloging, but using them as hard RS cuts is model-dependent.  A faithful
constraint must specify the dilepton \(q^2\) regions, charmonium vetoes,
form-factor inputs, nonlocal charm treatment, correlations with \(C_7\) from
\(b\to s\gamma\), and correlations with \(R_K\) and \(B\to K^*\ell\ell\).
The entry is therefore a semileptonic \(\Delta B=1\) constraint seed, not a
drop-in replacement for a global \(b\to s\ell\ell\) fit.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The modern phenomenology surface lists only
\(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) systems at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.  The only live
lepton-flavor module is \(\mu\to e\gamma\), implemented at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.  Required greps over the
implementation directories found no \(B\to K\ell\ell\), \(b\to s\ell\ell\),
\(C_9\), \(C_{10}\), or \(R_K\) backend.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Integration would require a new \(\Delta B=1\) semileptonic
Hamiltonian, matching to \(C_7\), \(C_9\), \(C_{10}\), and primed operators,
\(q^2\)-bin
handling, form factors, nonlocal-charm uncertainties, and experimental
covariance support.  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) basis is not enough.
